tasks.prompt: Photorealistic 3/4 angle photograph of a 2014 Ford Transit 350 XLT cargo van — high-roof variant, extended-length (long wheelbase, 148" wheelbase). Boxy rear corners, swept-forward A-pillar, Ford-style grille shape. NOT a Mercedes Sprinter. Camera positioned at the rear-right corner showing the passenger side and rear cargo doors at roughly 60/40 visibility. Eye-level, single subject filling roughly 70% of the frame. The entire vehicle body is wrapped in ultra-matte deep black vinyl (#0a0a0a) — completely flat, no specular highlights, no chrome trim, no glossy reflections, like 3M 2080-M12 matte vinyl. A thin warm gold (#c8943e) horizontal pinstripe runs the full length of the side just below the upper third. On the upper third of the passenger side: a clean blank rectangular cream-colored panel (#e8e0d4), 8 feet wide by 2 feet tall, ready for wordmark overlay — show as smooth flat ivory rectangle, NO text, NO letters, NO words. The rear cargo doors show: a smaller cream rectangular zone near the top (2 feet tall, centered), then below it a 4-by-2 grid of small square cream tiles (8 inches each with 2-inch gutters), then two square cream tiles in the bottom corners (4 inches each, 12 inches in from the outside edge). All cream zones are completely BLANK — placeholder panels, no markings whatsoever. Setting: parked on a gravel lot in front of a weathered wooden juke joint at dusk. Single warm sodium streetlamp lighting from upper left casts long shadows. Mississippi cypress trees in soft-focus background. Cinematic warm shadows and cool highlights, mid-grain 35mm film aesthetic, shot on Kodak Portra 400 with a 35mm lens at f/4 ISO 800. Mood: rugged, lived-in, classic American touring van, ready to drive 1000 miles.

tasks.prompt: Editorial-style photorealistic full side profile photograph of a 2014 Ford Transit 350 XLT cargo van — high-roof, extended length (long wheelbase). Clearly Ford Transit silhouette with squared-off rear corners and forward-leaning A-pillar — NOT a Mercedes Sprinter. Camera perfectly perpendicular to the passenger side, captured with telephoto compression (85mm equivalent). Full vehicle in frame, parked parallel to the camera. The entire body is wrapped in ultra-matte deep black vinyl (#0a0a0a), completely non-reflective. A thin warm gold (#c8943e) horizontal pinstripe runs the full length of the side. The upper-third of the side body has a clean blank rectangular cream-colored panel (#e8e0d4), 8 feet wide by 2 feet tall, completely flat and unmarked — NO text, NO letters, NO logos, just a smooth ivory rectangle ready for post-production overlay. Setting: parked at a gravel pull-off on a high bluff overlooking the wide Mississippi River during the last 20 minutes of golden hour. Warm low-angle sunlight from camera left. Sky is a soft gradient from pale gold to dusty rose. River below in soft focus, suggested by atmospheric haze. Editorial color grade, slightly saturated warm tones, subtle film grain. Shot on Kodak Portra 400. Composition is clean and editorial — the van is the subject, the bluff and river are atmospheric context.

tasks.prompt: Photorealistic straight-on rear photograph of a 2014 Ford Transit 350 XLT cargo van — high-roof, extended-length. Camera positioned dead-centered behind the vehicle at a slightly low angle (camera height around 4 feet), at moderate distance (~15 feet). Both rear cargo doors fully visible. The entire body is wrapped in ultra-matte deep black vinyl (#0a0a0a) — completely flat, no shine, no chrome, no specular reflections. The rear cargo doors show three distinct blank cream-colored placeholder zones: (1) a horizontal cream rectangular panel near the top, 3 feet wide by 2 feet tall, centered across the door pair seam; (2) below it, a 4-by-2 grid of small square cream tiles (8 inches each, with 2-inch gutters between them, centered); (3) two square cream tiles in the bottom corners (4 inches each, positioned 12 inches in from the outside edge of each door). ALL cream zones are completely blank smooth ivory panels — NO text, NO letters, NO numbers, NO logos, NO QR codes, no markings of any kind. They are placeholder panels for post-production overlay. Setting: parked in front of a plain corrugated-metal warehouse exterior, neutral grey wall background, even diffuse daylight with no harsh shadows. Clean documentation-quality lighting suitable for verifying wrap layout. Shot on a full-frame DSLR with a 50mm lens at f/8 ISO 200. Sharp focus front-to-back, neutral white balance, minimal color grading.
